channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: PlayStation Store’s Deals Tab Has Disappeared
item.content:encoded:
For the first time in a very long time, the ‘Deals’ tab on the PlayStation Store has gone missing.

For those who don’t know, Sony is currently facing a class action lawsuit that would require it to pay about $7.8 million to all U.S. residents who purchased specific digital games through the PlayStation Network. Since then, the first new development for the store has arrived, and it’s one that some may never have seen before.

PlayStation Store Doesn’t Have a Deals Tab Right Now

Spotted by PushSquare, if you head over to the PlayStation Store on your console, in the mobile app, or on the website, you will see that the Deals tab is no longer there. The Deals tab has always been filled with discounts for PS4 and PS5 games, no matter how minuscule, and it’s just strange to not see that tab anymore. A sale was planned for today, May 7; however, it seems Sony may have decided against it.

The report also talks about another reason that could have resulted in the ‘Deals’ tabs disappearing. PlayStation has been working on UI changes for its current-gen console, which could mean it is also reworking the PlayStation Store. However, that change would’ve appeared in a beta version rather than the standard version, so it might be that the store doesn’t have any active deals at the moment.

In other news, here’s how you can check eligibility and claim money from the recent Sony PlayStation lawsuit. Additionally, the PlayStation Plus free monthly games for May 2026 have been revealed, and they include a very popular sports title. What are your thoughts on the PlayStation Store’s ‘Deals’ tab disappearing? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the patch notes for the GTA Online Nightclub Bonuses update. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: GTA Online Nightclub Bonuses Update & Patch Notes—2X GTA$, RP & More
item.content:encoded:
GTA Online’s latest update focuses on Nightclub Management, doubles payouts for missions and jobs, and offers discounts on vehicles and missions.

As we wait for GTA 6 to release, it still isn’t time to sunset their long-running multiplayer series GTA Online. The title is still receiving constant support with Rockstar recently teasing an “exciting” update for Summer 2026. However, for now, let’s focus on the Nightclub Bonuses update.

What’s New in GTA Online Nightclub Bonuses Update

First and foremost, players will now receive 2X GTA$ in Daily Income from owned clubs after the GTA Online Nightclub Bonuses update, and property values have been dropped by 40%. Additionally, Nightclub Warehouse Stock production speed has been increased with X GTA$, RP, and Nightclub Goods through Business Batters or doing Nightclub Goods Missions with Yohan.

Moreover, players will receive 2X GTA$, RP, and Popularity on Nightclub Management Missions, 2X GTA$ and RP on Terrorbyte Client Jobs, 2X GTA$ and RP on Deadline, and Triple Rewards on the Community Mission Series. Check out the Vehicle and Weapon Discounts in the Patch Notes below:

All GTA Online Vehicle and Weapon Discounts Revealed in Nightclub Bonuses Patch Notes

Vehicle Discounts

  • Grotti Itali GTO (Sports) – 30% off 
  • Western Company Cargobob (Helicopter) – 30% off 
  • Karin Boor (Off-Road) – 30% off 
  • Willard Eudora (Muscle) – 30% off 
  • Pfister 811 (Super) – 30% off 
  • Declasse Drift Tampa (Sports) – 30% off 
  • Grotti Stinger GT (Sports Classic) – 30% off 
  • Übermacht Sentinel Classic (Sports) – 30% off 
  • Canis Freecrawler (Off-Road) – 30% off 

Weapon Discounts

  • Combat Shotgun | Heavy Rifle (30% off)
  • Compact EMP Launcher (30% off for GTA+ Members)
  • Sniper Rifle 
  • Up-n-Atomizer
  • The Shocker
  • Pipe Bombs
  • Tear Gas
  • Proximity Mines

The new bonuses and discounts were revealed in Rockstar’s latest blog post.

In other news, it was revealed that one GTA Shark Card transaction was worth $1 million in 2020. Additionally,  PlayStation 4 leads Xbox Series X in GTA Online users, with PC ranking last. What are your thoughts on the GTA Online Nightclub Bonuses update? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about no new information about Project Helix being revealed today. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: All LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Pre-Order Bonuses, Editions & Prices, Explained
item.content:encoded:
It’s never been more difficult to place an order for upcoming games, thanks to how many editions each one tends to have! The same can be said for LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, which has numerous editions on offer.

Now, not only do you need to decide whether you want the game or not, but you also need to figure out which edition to go for! Thankfully, LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight doesn’t have too many to pick from, but they might still sway you. Here’s what’s on offer, as well as how much each edition costs.

How Much Does LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Cost?

The standard edition of LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will cost the usual $69.99, as it runs the same price as the usual AAA title.

It might be easy to look at it and think it’s ‘just a LEGO game,’ but these titles have come on leaps and bounds since they first began. Now, there’s a huge open world to explore and Arkham-oriented combat to master: LEGO games have never been more AAA than this.

  • More Insider Gaming: LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Game Pass & Early Access Status, Explained

Partner that with a healthy runtime for the game, and in today’s market, it looks like LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight could be very well worth its price tag. But if you’re looking for some extras, there are a few goodies up for grabs, including some pre-order bonuses.

Does LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Have Pre-Order Bonuses?

Yes, LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight does have pre-order bonuses, though they depend on which edition of the game you pre-order.

There are two editions of LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight on offer:

  • Standard Edition
  • Digital Deluxe Edition

Pre-ordering either of them will net you the Dark Knight Returns Batsuit, which is inspired by the legendary comic book series. However, if you pre-order the Digital Deluxe Edition, not only will you get the suit, but you’ll also be granted 72-hours of early access to the full game!

That means you’ll be able to play the game three days earlier than everyone else, with a launch date of May 19 instead of May 22. So, if you’re desperate to dive into Gotham and play through the Caped Crusader’s latest adventure, that’s the edition that you’ll want to pre-order, but it comes with a host of other bonuses, too—details can be found below.

Every LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight Edition—All Contents, Explained

As mentioned above, there are two editions of LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, with the Digital Deluxe Edition fetching the highest price, at $89.99.

Since it’s digital-only, don’t expect a snazzy statue or art booklet to keep you entertained—there are real-life LEGO sets for that! Instead, you’ll get access to some cool add-ons in-game, including an eventual release for two new playable characters: Harley Quinn and the Joker!

  • More Insider Gaming: Is LEGO Batman: Legacy Of The Dark Knight 2 Player? Co-Op & Multiplayer, Explained

For full details, check out everything that’s included in the Digital Deluxe Edition below:

  • Full Base Game
  • Legacy Collection (available at launch)
    • Arkham Trilogy Pack – Seven new suits (one per original playable character), one new Batmobile, and a set of five Batcave customization props.
    • Batman Beyond Pack- Seven new suits (one per original playable character), one new Batmobile, and a set of five Batcave customization props.
    • Party Music Pack – Seven new suits (one per original playable character), one new Batmobile, and a set of five Batcave customization props.
  • Mayhem Collection (available September 2026)
    • New Mayhem Mode featuring The Joker and Harley Quinn as playable characters with their own abilities, gadgets, and takedowns.
    • New Story Mission with The Joker and Harley Quinn breaking out of Arkham Asylum and causing chaos on the streets of Gotham City.
    • Sinister Pack – Seven new suits (one per original playable character), one new Batmobile, and a set of five Batcave customization props.

Which edition of LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight will you be purchasing? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more Insider Gaming content, check out the following:

  • Sony PlayStation Lawsuit Refund: How To Check Eligibility and Claim Your Money
  • Invincible VS Complete Character Tier List
  • All Free Characters in Neverness to Everness & How to Get Them

Charlie is one of Insider Gaming's Guides Writers who's been in the gaming industry for a little over a year. Unfortunately for him, he enjoys every video game genre out…

More from Charlie Champion

MOBILE APP

item.title: LEGO Batman Launch Trailer Features Seal’s ‘Kiss From a Rose’
item.content:encoded:
LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight‘s launch trailer features more blasts from the past, including the iconic Seal song from the 1995 classic Batman Forever.

The game, slated for release later this month, honors multiple iterations of the bat. Thus far, it’s known that there will be multiple outfits for all characters, with more than 100 different suits to collect. Now, with the game releasing in Early Access in about 2 weeks for those who pre-ordered the Deluxe Edition, the launch trailer shows references to the Dark Knight Christopher Nolan movies and other theatrical Batman releases, including the aforementioned Batman Forever.

LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight Shows Split-Screen Co-Op and More

This trailer is completely dedicated to the interpretations of the movie releases. The build-up to yet another instance of Martha and Thomas Wayne being murdered in Crime Alley was shown. Also, the story of Poison Ivy from the 1997 film Batman and Robin was shown. Speaking of Robin, split-screen co-op gameplay was also shown. All of this as Seal’s song plays over the action.

Jonathan Smith, Head of Development at TT Games, previously discussed the team’s thought process when adapting some of the gameplay from Rocksteady’s legendary Arkham series.

“What we find with all our LEGO games is that the more we can draw upon different aspects of a character, the wider we can make our gameplay, the more appealing it is to us as players, and to younger players and older players.”

Read more of his comments here.

For more LEGO Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight, check out the PC specs of the upcoming game. LEGO Batman releases globally on May 22, 2026. Insider Gaming will continue to provide the latest information surrounding the release.

How excited are you for this game? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

item.title: Rockstar Games Teasing ‘Exciting’ GTA Online Summer Update
item.content:encoded:
Rockstar Games has planned an exciting new update planned for GTA Online in Summer 2026.

There’s still a bit of time left until the most anticipated game of today’s day and age, GTA 6, releases. However, work on the previous title continues as we await the next mainline entry. It seems Rockstar has some surprises left for GTA Online before the world moves over to GTA 6 in November 2026.

Rockstar Discusses Upcoming Stuff for GTA Online

GTA Online’s exciting summer update was mentioned in Rockstar’s latest blog post. The new post features an “Upcoming Improvements and Adjustments” section, where the devs discuss how they are continually working to make GTA Online a better experience. This includes rebalancing and targeting cheaters and other players who negatively impact the game. The developers mentioned that the base payouts for certain jobs will increase next week.

Moreover, the developers are also adjusting vehicle prices and chasing down players who abuse vehicle resale exploits by limiting vehicle resale values. Towards the very end, Rockstar confirmed that this isn’t the end and there’s a lot more coming to GTA Online ahead of GTA 6’s release, “including a variety of special events and celebrations, along with an exciting new update this summer.” No other information on this update was revealed.

In other news, PlayStation 4 leads Xbox Series X in GTA Online users, with PC ranking last. Additionally, it was revealed that one GTA Shark Card transaction was worth $1 million in 2020. What are your thoughts on GTA Online’s exciting summer update? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about no new information about Project Helix being revealed today. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: No New Xbox Project Helix Information Is Expected Today
item.content:encoded:
The Xbox Game Dev Update event today doesn’t feature any new information on their next-gen console, Project Helix.

Towards the end of last month, it was revealed that Microsoft will host a Game Dev Update on May 7, where players will receive a “closer look at Project Helix.” However, it seems that wasn’t the case at all, and the new information regarding their next-gen console will instead arrive later this year.

Xbox Project Helix Event Only Features a Recap from GDC

The VP for Next Generation at Xbox, Jason Ronald, has confirmed on X (formerly Twitter) that no new information about Project Helix is being revealed today. The Game Dev Update event will focus solely on a recap of announcements made earlier this year at the Game Developer Conference 2026 for those who were unable to attend. New details about Project Helix will be shared later this year; however, no date was provided.

Jason Ronald revealed quite a few details about the next-gen console at GDC 2026, and here’s what we know: The console will be powered by a custom AMD SOC, which has been co-designed for the next generation of DirectX, features next-gen Ray Tracing performance and capabilities, and brings GPU direct work graph execution.

It also comes with AMD FSR next, which is built for the next generation of Neural Rendering and ML upscaling. It also brings ML multi-frame generation and next-gen ray generation for RT and path tracing. Lastly, the console features Deep Texture compression with Neural Texture Compression and DirectStorage & Zstd. It was also revealed at the event that devs will receive alpha versions of the console in 2027.

In other news, the Xbox CEO has confirmed that the memory shortage will impact Project Helix pricing and availability. Additionally, Ronald has also clarified on first-party rumors. What are your thoughts on Xbox not providing any new information on Project Helix today? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Diablo Immortal’s StarCraft crossover content. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP
